rules.name: Judge_Richter
rules.description: Captures full person names following 'Richter' or 'Richterin', including titles like Mag., Dr., Prof., Hon.-Prof., VetR, etc.

rules.name: Complainant_Beschwerdesache
rules.description: Captures person names following 'in der Beschwerdesache', handling various titles and name structures.

rules.name: Frau_Herr_Name
rules.description: Captures person names following 'Frau' or 'Herr', including titles and degree suffixes.

rules.name: Person_After_Verb
rules.description: Captures person names appearing after verbs like 'hat', 'brachte', 'kündigte', 'beschloss' in legal contexts, often followed by 'an', 'ein', or a comma.

rules.name: Techn_R_Title
rules.description: Captures 'Techn R' followed by a name. Updated to match the full title and name.

rules.name: OSR_Title_Name
rules.description: Captures names preceded by the title 'OSR' (Oberster Staatsanwalt/Rechtsanwalt).

rules.name: Dative_Bei_Der_Name
rules.description: Captures person names appearing after 'bei der' (dative case), common in legal texts for service of process.

rules.name: Name_After_Comma_With_Address
rules.description: Captures person names after a comma, strictly requiring a following address pattern (number + street) to avoid matching common nouns or titles.

rules.name: Name_After_Comma_Address
rules.description: Captures person names after a comma, strictly requiring a following address pattern (number + street) to avoid matching common nouns or titles.

rules.name: Name_After_Verb
rules.description: Captures person names appearing after verbs like 'hat', 'brachte', 'kündigte', 'beschloss' in legal contexts. Excludes 'Herr' from the capture group if it appears as a title before the name.

rules.name: Name_After_Haftung
rules.description: Captures person names appearing after 'Haftung des' (liability of) to catch cases like 'Haftung des Felix Stukenbröker'.

rules.name: VetR_Title_Name
rules.description: Captures names preceded by 'VetR' (Veterinär).

rules.name: StR_Title_Name
rules.description: Captures names preceded by 'StR' (Strafrecht).

rules.name: HR_Title_Name
rules.description: Captures names preceded by 'HR' (Hofrat).

rules.name: Hon_Prof_in_Title_Name
rules.description: Captures names preceded by 'Hon.-Prof.in'.

rules.name: Gegen_Name
rules.description: Captures person names appearing after 'gegen' (against). Handles titles and standalone names. Uses capture group to extract only the name.

rules.name: Legal_Context_Name
rules.description: Captures person names in 'in der Beschwerdesache' context. Handles names followed by comma or address.

rules.name: Party_Name_In_Context
rules.description: Captures person names in 'in der Beschwerdesache' context. Uses capture group to extract only the name, excluding the context prefix.

rules.name: Name_At_Start_Of_Text
rules.description: Captures a person name at the very beginning of the text, followed by a comma and address. Handles cases like 'René Kalcher, ...'.

rules.name: Name_At_Start_Of_Text_Suffix
rules.description: Captures a person name at the start with suffixes, followed by comma and address.

rules.name: Party_Name_In_Context_Suffix
rules.description: Captures person names in genitive context with suffixes, followed by a comma.

rules.name: Legal_Context_Name_Suffix
rules.description: Captures person names in 'in der Beschwerdesache' context with suffixes, followed by a comma. Uses capture group to extract only the name.

rules.name: Name_After_Comma_Flexible
rules.description: Captures person names appearing after a comma, followed by a verb, preposition, or end of sentence. Excludes addresses and common nouns. Handles genitive articles.

rules.name: Bei_Name_Context
rules.description: Captures person names appearing after 'bei' (dative case), common in legal texts for service of process or location.

rules.name: Techn_R_Suffix_Name
rules.description: Captures 'Techn R' followed by a name and potential academic suffixes (BEd, Bakk, etc.).

rules.name: Gegen_Name_Full
rules.description: Captures person names appearing after 'gegen' (against). Handles titles and standalone names. Uses capture group to extract only the name.

rules.name: Name_After_Verb_Enhanced
rules.description: Captures person names appearing after verbs like 'hat', 'brachte', 'kündigte', 'beschloss' in legal contexts. Excludes 'Herr' from the capture group if it appears as a title before the name.

rules.name: Priv_Doz_in_Title_Name
rules.description: Captures names preceded by 'Priv.-Doz.in'.

rules.name: Name_After_Antrag
rules.description: Captures person names appearing after 'Antrag des' or 'Antrag der', requiring a comma or specific context to avoid false positives.

rules.name: Mag_a_Title_Name
rules.description: Captures names preceded by 'Mag.a' (female Magister) as a standalone title or after other titles.

rules.name: Name_After_Genitive_Context
rules.description: Captures person names following genitive articles 'der/des' in legal contexts (e.g., 'der Beschwerdesache [Name]', 'des [Name] u. Mitbes.'), ensuring they are followed by a comma, specific legal markers, or end of phrase.

rules.name: Name_With_Suffix_Generic
rules.description: Captures person names followed by academic/professional suffixes. Strictly requires word boundaries and excludes common legal nouns.

rules.name: Name_After_Comma_Suffix
rules.description: Captures person names after a comma, including specific academic suffixes, strictly requiring a following address pattern (number + street) to avoid matching common nouns or titles.

rules.name: Name_After_Address_Context
rules.description: Captures person names appearing after a comma, followed by a street name and number (address pattern), to catch names without suffixes like 'Elfriede Billmeyer'.

rules.name: Name_After_Preposition_Generic
rules.description: Captures person names after common prepositions ONLY if followed by a comma and address pattern. Fixed to exclude context words from the capture group.

rules.name: Name_After_Beschwerdesache
rules.description: Captures person names following 'Beschwerdesache' (or similar) in various contexts. Fixed to ensure the capture group starts after the context word and excludes company names.

rules.name: Name_After_Beschwerdesache_Simple
rules.description: Captures names after 'Beschwerdesache' if not followed by an address immediately, but requires a comma or end of phrase. Fixed to exclude context words.

rules.name: Name_After_Vertraten_Durch
rules.description: Captures person names after 'vertreten durch'. Excludes job titles and company names. Requires a name pattern (Capitalized word) immediately following.

rules.name: PreNominal_Title_Name
rules.description: Captures person names preceded by complex legal/academic titles, handling consecutive titles and gender suffixes.

rules.name: Name_After_Gender_Title
rules.description: Captures names preceded by 'Herr' or 'Frau' (including genitive 'Herrn'/'Frau') to catch cases like 'Herrn Hermine Burgschweiger' or 'Herr und Herta Birkenbihl'. Excludes non-names and ensures full capture with suffixes.
